description: Sylarien's amphitheater is carved from the crown of the city's broadest sky-oak, its living-wood seats arranged in ascending tiers around a central stage that seems to float in the open air. The acoustics are legendary: a whisper on stage carries to the highest seat, amplified by the natural resonance of the living wood and enhanced by Wind-element channeling that Windspeaker Liora Airsong personally calibrated. When Liora sings here, her Kin-sense makes every listener's own resonance vibrate in sympathy, creating a shared emotional experience that transcends mere entertainment. The amphitheater seats three thousand and is the cultural heart of all three canopy cities.

name: Sylarien - Guild Compact Hall
description: The Guild Compact Hall is Sylarien's seat of governance, a building whose appearance changes with each guild's term in the rotating leadership. Currently the Weavers' Guild holds the chair, and the hall is draped in their signature wind-silk tapestries depicting the history of Sylarien's founding. The Compact system allows each major artisan guild to govern for a season, ensuring that no single craft dominates the city's priorities. Windspeaker Liora Airsong serves as the permanent voice of the Compact, her Pasua gift for oratory and song making her the natural mediator between competing guild interests.

name: Sylarien - Loom District
description: The Loom District is Sylarien's industrial heart, where the living looms produce wind-silk and dyed fabrics in quantities that rival Liraveth's output. The looms themselves are remarkable: frames of living wood that the weavers say respond to their moods, tightening or loosening the weave in ways that produce fabrics of almost supernatural quality. The click and shuttle of the looms creates a rhythm that the weavers work to, their hands moving in time with a beat that Kin-sense users perceive as a blend of craft and elemental resonance. Dyes extracted from sky-oak bark, mountain minerals, and cultivated flowers produce colors that remain vivid for decades.

name: Sylarien - Instrument Crafting Workshop
description: Sylarien's woodwind instruments are prized throughout Twin Rivers and beyond, their clear tones attributed to the combination of sky-oak wood, Hasura craftsmanship, and the unique acoustic properties of wood shaped at canopy height. The workshop produces flutes, pipes, and the distinctive multi-chambered wind-harps that are Sylarien's signature instrument. Each piece is tested on the workshop's small stage, and the sound of scales and experimental melodies drifts from the workshop at all hours. The master craftsman, an elderly Hasura with fingers worn smooth by decades of wood-shaping, can produce an instrument in a week that will last a lifetime.

name: Sylarien - Northern Platform
description: The northern platform of Sylarien overlooks the forest canopy stretching toward Aerithal, and the bridge to that city is visible as a graceful arc against the sky. This district houses more of the city's practical infrastructure: food stores, water collection systems, and the wind-powered grain mills that process imported cereals. A small Pekakarlik engineering office maintains the structural integrity of the platforms, and a Tanuki weather station provides forecasts based on a combination of Wind-element reading and the Tanuki's inexplicable intuition about storms. The platform is breezy and open, with wide views in every direction.

name: Sylarien - Artisan Row
description: A section of the promenade where Sylarien's diverse artisan guilds maintain their shopfronts and demonstration workshops. Dyers hang freshly colored wind-silk from every available line, creating curtains of color that billow and shift in the breeze. Woodcarvers produce decorative panels and musical instruments. Pasua storytellers perform miniature dramas for passing crowds, their voices carrying the distinctive resonance of a people whose art and Kin-sense are inseparable. The row is Sylarien in miniature: creative, colorful, and humming with the energy of people who define themselves by what they make.

name: Sylarien - Pasua Quarter
description: The Pasua Quarter is the cultural heartbeat of Sylarien, home to the Pasua Elves whose singing, storytelling, and musical traditions have shaped the city's identity. Buildings here are adorned with sound-sculptures that transform wind into music, and the walls of homes bear carved narratives that tell stories in visual form. Windspeaker Liora Airsong's personal residence occupies a central platform, recognizable by the wind-harps mounted on its roof that play constantly in the breeze. The Kin-sense resonance of the quarter is extraordinary: warm, musical, and so rich with emotional resonance that visitors often find themselves moved to tears or laughter without understanding why.

name: Sylarien - Dye Works
description: The Dye Works occupy a series of platforms on the city's western edge, where the prevailing winds carry the chemical vapors away from the residential districts. Vats of dye in every imaginable color bubble and steam, tended by dyers whose hands and arms are permanently stained in the rainbow hues of their craft. The dyes are produced from sky-oak bark, mineral pigments imported from Stoneharbor, and botanical extracts from Timberdeep, creating a palette that is uniquely Sylarien. The most skilled dyers can produce fabrics that seem to change color with the viewer's mood, a technique that blends craft skill with subtle Kin-sense manipulation.

name: Sylarien - Tanuki Bazaar
description: The Tanuki community in Sylarien has established a bazaar that is half marketplace and half performance space. Stalls sell curiosities, clever toys, and the joke-trinkets that Tanuki artisans produce with loving mischief, while Tanuki performers entertain the crowds with illusions, acrobatics, and the absurdist comedy that is their people's art form. The Wild Static here is cheerful and chaotic, making wind-silk flutter in impossible patterns and causing lanterns to flash in rainbow sequences. Sylarien's residents consider the Tanuki Bazaar essential to the city's creative ecosystem, the controlled chaos feeding inspiration.

name: Sylarien - Southern Residential
description: The southern residential platforms of Sylarien house the city's mixed population of Hasura artisans, Human craftworkers, and the various specialists who keep the canopy city functioning. Homes here show the influence of the Guild Compact in their decoration: each building bears the symbols of its inhabitants' guild affiliations, and the seasonal governance rotation is reflected in the way decorations change with each new guild's term. The platforms look south over the forest canopy, and on quiet evenings the distant sound of the Amphitheater carries across the residential district, a reminder that in Sylarien, art is never far away.

name: Sylarien - Western Bridge Head
description: The western edge of Sylarien terminates at a bridge head that connects to the Aerithal canopy bridge via a series of intermediate platforms. The bridge descends slightly before climbing toward Aerithal's higher canopy, and the crossing offers the most dramatic views of any inter-city route: the forest floor three hundred feet below, the canopy stretching in every direction, and the sky above where Aerithal's observatory spire is visible against the clouds. A wind-silk canopy shelters waiting travelers, and a posting board lists bridge conditions and scheduled crossings.

name: Great River Road - South of Custos
description: The Great River Road leads south from Custos do Aeternos through rolling hills where farms spread across the rich bottomland on both sides of the Great River. The road is wide and well-maintained, packed earth reinforced with river gravel, and grain wagons are a constant presence heading north to the capital. Willows line the riverbank to the east, their trailing branches creating green curtains that flutter in the river breeze. The Water element is strong along the banks, a cool, flowing resonance that accompanies travelers like a quiet companion.

name: Great River Road - Farmlands
description: The road passes through the heart of Twin Rivers' agricultural belt, where wheat fields stretch golden to the horizon on both sides. Farm buildings of timber and thatch dot the landscape, and the distant figures of field workers move among the grain. The Great River is visible to the east as a silver thread between the willows, and barges loaded with grain drift north toward Custos. The air smells of ripe wheat, warm earth, and the faintly sweet scent of river water. Milestone markers show the distance to Greenford decreasing with each step south.

name: Great River Road - River Bend
description: The road curves to follow the Great River as it bends westward, the river widening here into a broad, slow stretch where fishing boats bob at anchor. A wayside inn with a thatched roof and a weathered signboard offers rest to travelers, its common room smelling of river fish stew and barley bread. The river bend creates a natural harbor where barges tie up overnight, their crews sharing the inn with road travelers. The light here is soft, filtered through the mist that perpetually rises from the slow-moving water.

name: Great River Road - Greenford Approaches
description: The road straightens as it approaches Greenford, the farms becoming more frequent and the traffic heavier. Grain carts converge from side roads, and the distant sound of millstones grinding is the first sign of the town ahead. The Great River runs close to the road here, and the combined smell of flour dust and river water is unmistakable. Road-side vendors sell water and fruit to travelers making the last stretch, and the whitewashed towers of Greenford's grain silos are visible above the treetops ahead.

name: Great River Road - Between Towns
description: The road between Greenford and Forkmeet follows the Great River through increasingly wooded terrain, the farms giving way to forest that crowds close on both sides. The river is audible but not always visible through the trees, and the road surface shows less maintenance than the northern sections. Timber wagons heading north from Forkmeet share the road with grain barges being towed upstream by draft teams on the towpath. A carved stone marks the halfway point between the two towns, its surface worn smooth by the hands of travelers who touch it for luck.

name: Great River Road - Forkmeet Approach
description: The road descends toward Forkmeet as the valley opens and the convergence of the Great River and Highwater River becomes visible ahead. The sound of two rivers meeting fills the air with a deep, constant rush that grows louder with each step. Timber rafts are visible on both rivers, and the smoke from Forkmeet's chimneys rises above the trees ahead. The Water element intensifies noticeably here, a doubled resonance that water-attuned Kin describe as feeling their senses sharpen and expand. The road surface improves as Forkmeet's maintenance begins.

name: Highwater River Road - Forkmeet East
description: The road east from Forkmeet follows the Highwater River upstream, climbing gradually through a valley that narrows between rocky hills. The Highwater runs fast and clear beside the road, its cold mountain water a sharp contrast to the Great River's warmer flow. Birch and alder line the banks, their roots gripping the rocky soil, and the occasional rapids send spray across the road in windy weather. The transition from river valley to hill country is marked by the changing birdsong and the cooling air.

name: Highwater River Road - The Narrows
description: The valley narrows dramatically here, the hills pressing close on both sides and the Highwater River channeled into a deep, swift passage between rock walls. The road is cut into the hillside above the water, wide enough for single-file traffic only, and the roar of the confined river makes conversation difficult. This stretch is impassable during spring flood, when the Highwater rises to cover the road entirely. Carved warnings in Pekakarlik script advise travelers to check water levels before attempting the passage. Beyond the narrows, the valley opens again toward Stoneharbor.

name: Highwater River Road - Stoneharbor Approach
description: The road widens as the valley opens before Stoneharbor, and the first signs of the mining town appear: slag heaps, ore cart tracks crossing the road, and the orange glow of forge fires visible in the bluffs ahead. The Highwater runs deep and dark here, its surface reflecting the stone walls of the bluffs that rise steeply on both sides. The smell of forge smoke and hot metal carries on the breeze, and the distant ring of hammers on anvils provides a metallic counterpoint to the river's rush. Stoneharbor's River Bluff Gate is visible ahead, carved into the cliff face.

name: Highwater River Road - Mountain Fork
description: Above Stoneharbor the road forks, one branch continuing to follow the Highwater upstream while another turns south toward the Riverwind Heights. A signpost carved in Pekakarlik script indicates distances to Highspire, the Riverwind Heights druidic site, and back to Stoneharbor. The air is cooler here, the mountain influence strengthening as the road climbs. The Highwater narrows to a rushing stream, its banks lined with stunted pines and the grey-green scrub of the mountain foothills.

name: Highwater River Road - Upper Valley
description: The upper Highwater valley is a wild, beautiful stretch where the river tumbles over granite boulders in a series of cascades. The road climbs steeply, switchbacking up the valley wall as the terrain transitions from hill country to true mountains. Mountain goats watch from the ridgeline above, and hawks circle in the thermals that rise from the sun-warmed rock faces. The road is maintained but rough, better suited to foot and pack animal than wagon traffic. Ahead, the Whispering Slopes begin where the murmurs of ancient Giant wisdom become audible to those with Kin-sense.

name: Riverwind Heights - Trail Head
description: A trail branches west from the mountain road toward the Riverwind Heights, a series of moss-covered hills that rise above the Highwater valley west of Stoneharbor. The hills are green and rounded, their shapes softened by centuries of wind and rain into forms that druids consider sacred. The trail is marked with Circle of Deeproot symbols carved into stone posts, indicating that the Heights are under druidic protection. The view from here encompasses the Highwater valley and Stoneharbor's bluff-side buildings, smoke rising from the forges like grey ribbons.

name: Riverwind Heights - Mossy Ridge
description: The trail climbs to the ridge of the Riverwind Heights, where ankle-deep moss covers every surface in a carpet of emerald green. The hills here are dotted with ancient standing stones that predate every settlement in Twin Rivers, their purpose debated by scholars but their druidic significance unquestioned. The wind carries a loamy, green scent mixed with the mineral tang of the river far below. Druids from the Circle of Deeproot maintain a ritual site here, and the stones are often adorned with fresh offerings of herbs and carved wooden tokens. The Kin-sense resonance is Earth-dominant, deep and patient, with an undercurrent of Water from the rivers below.

name: Riverwind Heights - Druidic Overlook
description: The highest point of the Riverwind Heights offers a panoramic view of the Twin Rivers basin: the Great River and Highwater visible as silver threads winding through green valleys, the settlements of Stoneharbor, Forkmeet, and distant Greenford identifiable by their smoke plumes. The Circle of Deeproot has placed a permanent ward-stone here, its surface carved with elemental sigils that druids use as a focus for weather-reading and long-distance communication. On still mornings, the ritual site resonates with a deep hum that druids say is the voice of the land itself, speaking through the rivers and stone that form Twin Rivers' bones.

name: Whispering Slopes - Lower Path
description: The path from Forkmeet climbs into the Whispering Slopes, a range of wind-scored hills between the river valley and the mountain approaches to Highspire. The name is literal: the wind through these hills carries sounds that some hear as murmured words, fragments of speech in a language that predates all known tongues. Scholars attribute the phenomenon to wind resonance through quartz-laced rock formations, but those with Kin-sense report perceiving structured thought in the whispers, as if Giants who once walked these slopes left their wisdom imprinted on the stone. The path is well-marked but unsettling, and locals do not travel it after dark.

name: Whispering Slopes - The Murmuring Pass
description: The pass through the heart of the Whispering Slopes is where the phenomenon is strongest. Winds funnel through a narrow gap between two hills, and the resulting sound is so clearly speech-like that travelers have been known to answer it before realizing no one spoke. The quartz veins in the rock glow faintly at dusk, and spectral presences have been reported by reliable witnesses: translucent figures of enormous size that walk the ridgeline and fade when approached. The Circle of Deeproot maintains ward-markers along the pass, not against the presences themselves, but against the disorientation that prolonged exposure to the whispers can cause.

name: Whispering Slopes - Upper Path
description: The upper slopes emerge from the most intense whispering zone into clearer air, the murmurs fading to a distant background hum. The terrain transitions here from the rounded, wind-sculpted hills of the Slopes to the harder rock of the mountain foothills that lead to Highspire. The path is steeper now, and the first mountain views appear: the Giant's Teeth visible as a jagged line against the northern sky, their peaks snow-capped even in summer. A waystone carved by Kovaka masons marks the boundary between the Slopes and the mountain approaches, its surface inscribed with both directions and a brief prayer for safe passage.

name: Whispering Slopes - Highspire Approach
description: The final stretch of road before Highspire's Mountain Gate climbs steeply along a ridge that offers views in every direction: the Whispering Slopes falling away to the west, the Giant's Teeth rising to the east, and the river valleys far below. The air is thin and cold, and the wind is persistent, carrying the clean scent of snow and pine from the peaks above. Mountain flowers bloom in sheltered crevices, their colors brilliant against the grey stone. The road narrows as it approaches the gate, the natural architecture of the mountain spur channeling travelers toward the settlement's single defensible entrance.

name: Singing Ridges - Western Approach
description: The path from Timberdeep emerges from the forest onto the Singing Ridges, a series of quartz-veined hills that once hummed with a melodic resonance attributed to Giant craftsmanship from the Age of Giants. The ridges are beautiful in a stark way, their surfaces pale with exposed quartz that sparkles in sunlight, and the wind that crosses them carries a quality of sound that is unique to this place. But something has changed: the singing that gave the ridges their name has fallen silent in recent seasons, a silence that the Circle of Deeproot considers deeply alarming. The path crosses the westernmost ridge, where druid deep-markers stand like sentinels on either side.

name: Singing Ridges - Crystal Hollow
description: A natural amphitheater in the central ridge where the quartz formations are densest and the acoustic properties most remarkable. When the ridges sang, this hollow was the epicenter, its crystalline walls amplifying the wind's melody into a harmonious chorus that could be heard for miles. Now the hollow is silent except for the ordinary whistle of wind through rock, and the absence is felt as a physical weight by those with Kin-sense. Druids have examined the crystal formations and found no physical change, which makes the silence more troubling, not less. Something in the elemental resonance has shifted, and no one can explain why.

name: Singing Ridges - Eastern Ridge
description: The easternmost Singing Ridge overlooks the deep forest that stretches toward Aerithal's canopy, the treetops visible as a dark green sea from this elevation. The ridge path descends through scrubby vegetation toward the Windweave Forest Road below, connecting the ridges to the network of paths between the canopy cities and ground-level settlements. From here, the full extent of the Singing Ridges is visible, a line of pale quartz hills running north to south through the darker landscape. The silence of the ridges is most striking from this vantage, where the wind should carry the sound of the Crystal Hollow's resonance but carries only empty air.

name: Singing Ridges - Wind Lord's Terrace
description: At the highest point of the central ridge stands a terrace of worked stone that is unmistakably Giant-crafted: its proportions too large for any mortal builder, its stonework too precise for anything but the greatest artisans of the Age of Giants. The terrace was built as an offering to the Wind Lord, and the Singing Ridges' resonance was designed to please that elemental power. The druid deep-markers here are the oldest in the region, their symbols worn but still legible, and the Circle of Deeproot treats this place with a reverence that borders on fear. The silence here is loudest: a void where something vast once sang.

name: Windweave Forest Road - Near Timberdeep
description: A ground-level path winds through the Windweave Forest between Timberdeep and the base camps of the canopy cities. The sky-oaks here are enormous, their trunks wider than houses and their canopy three hundred feet overhead, filtering the sunlight to a green-gold twilight. The forest floor is carpeted with moss and ferns, and the air is rich with the scent of decomposing leaves and living wood. The path is marked by druid carvings on the tree trunks, and the canopy above grows darker and thicker with each step deeper into the forest. Ground-level travelers report feeling small here, dwarfed by the scale of the ancient trees.

name: Windweave Forest Road - Deep Forest
description: The forest road enters a section where the canopy is so thick that the forest floor exists in perpetual twilight. Bioluminescent fungi provide faint blue-green light along the path, and the sounds of the upper canopy are muffled by the sheer mass of vegetation overhead. The sky-oaks here are among the oldest in the forest, their bark scarred by centuries and their roots creating a maze of ridges and hollows across the forest floor. The path connects to the base camps from which the canopy lift stations provide access to the cities above. The Kin-sense resonance is dense, layered, ancient: the combined life force of trees that have stood since before any settlement was founded.

name: Windweave Forest Road - Canopy Base Camp
description: A clearing in the deep forest serves as the base camp for travelers heading to the canopy cities. Simple shelters and a watering station are maintained here, and the massive cables and counterweight mechanisms of the Liraveth canopy lift are visible above, rising through the canopy like the rigging of some impossible ship. Ground-level traders store goods here for transport up, and descending travelers rest before continuing their journey. The clearing is one of the few places in the deep forest where direct sunlight reaches the ground, and the sudden brightness is almost startling after the twilight of the forest path.

name: Windweave Forest Road - Eastern Deep
description: The forest road continues east past the base camp into even older growth, where the sky-oaks give way in places to the rare ironwood trees whose dense timber is among the most valuable commodities in Twin Rivers. The path narrows as it penetrates deeper, and the sounds of the forest change: birdsong gives way to the creaking of ancient branches and the rustle of unseen creatures in the undergrowth. The Aetherial Veil is invisible from the forest floor here, blocked by the immense canopy, but its influence can be felt as a faint tingling pressure on Kin-sense. The road eventually connects to the approaches to Aerithal's ground station.

name: Mountain Approaches - Lower Pass
description: The mountain path from Highspire enters the Giant's Teeth through a narrow pass between two towering peaks. The rock here is volcanic basalt veined with obsidian, and the air carries a faint sulfurous tang from distant thermal vents. The pass is the primary route between Twin Rivers and the eastern regions, and despite its difficulty it sees regular traffic from traders, messengers, and the occasional adventurer heading deeper into the mountains. Stone cairns mark the path where snow might obscure it, and emergency shelters built into the rock face provide refuge from the sudden storms that sweep the peaks.

name: Mountain Approaches - Volcanic Shelf
description: A broad shelf of volcanic rock provides a resting point along the mountain pass, its surface warmed by thermal vents that make the stone comfortable to sit on even in winter. Sulfurous steam rises from cracks in the rock, and the air has a mineral bite that catches in the throat. The view from the shelf encompasses the pass in both directions, and on clear days the Twin Rivers basin is visible to the northwest as a patchwork of green and silver far below. Pekakarlik geologists have studied the thermal activity here and declared it stable, but the mountain has its own opinions about stability.

name: Mountain Approaches - High Pass
description: The highest point of the pass through the Giant's Teeth is a wind-blasted saddle between two peaks where the air is thin enough to leave lowlanders gasping. The wind is ferocious here, channeled by the peaks into a constant gale that has scoured the rock smooth. On the rare calm days, the silence is absolute, broken only by the distant cry of mountain eagles. The pass marks the boundary of Twin Rivers' territory, and a stone boundary marker carved in multiple languages announces this to travelers heading in either direction. Beyond lies the descent toward Kinsweave and the eastern regions.

name: Mountain Approaches - Eagle's Perch
description: A side path from the lower pass leads to a rocky outcropping known as Eagle's Perch, where mountain eagles nest in crevices and the view is said to be the finest in the Giant's Teeth. The perch overlooks the entire approach from the Twin Rivers side, and it was used as a signal station during the Deceivers' War. The stone here bears scorch marks from the signal fires, and rusted iron brackets still hold the remnants of a beacon platform. The eagles have reclaimed the perch since the war's end, and they regard human visitors with the imperious tolerance of creatures who know they own the sky.

name: Canopy Bridge - Liraveth to Sylarien
description: The canopy bridge between Liraveth and Sylarien is a masterwork of combined engineering, three hundred feet of living-wood walkway supported by Pekakarlik iron cables anchored to the mightiest sky-oaks of both groves. The bridge sways gently with the wind, its movement controlled by tension systems that prevent dangerous oscillation. The walk across takes several minutes and offers views that no ground-level traveler ever experiences: the forest canopy spreading in every direction like a green ocean, the rivers glinting far below, and the sky above close enough to touch. Wind-silk railings provide safety, and small shelters at the midpoint offer rest during storms.

name: Canopy Bridge - Sylarien East Span
description: The eastern span of the Sylarien bridge extends from the city toward the intermediate platform that connects to the Liraveth bridge network. This section of the bridge passes through the canopy of a grove of particularly old sky-oaks, and the branches form a natural tunnel that filters the wind and creates a sheltered passage. Birds nest in the bridge's structural members, adding their song to the ever-present sound of wind through the canopy. The living-wood walkway flexes perceptibly underfoot, a sensation that newcomers find alarming but residents describe as the bridge breathing.

name: Canopy Bridge - Liraveth to Aerithal
description: The bridge between Liraveth and Aerithal climbs as it extends northward, reflecting the higher elevation of Aerithal's canopy. The ascent is gradual but noticeable, and the bridge's construction compensates with a slight staircase quality to the walkway. The view during the crossing is breathtaking: the entire Windweave Forest canopy stretches below, and Aerithal's observatory spire is visible ahead, rising above the treetops like a finger pointing at the stars. The bridge is busy with traffic between the two cities' scholarly communities, and researchers crossing with armloads of scrolls and charts are a common sight.

name: Canopy Bridge - Aerithal South Landing
description: The final span of the Liraveth-Aerithal bridge arrives at a landing platform on Aerithal's southern edge, where the higher canopy makes the transition from bridge to city feel like ascending from one world to another. The air is cooler and cleaner at this elevation, and the constant wind carries the distinctive ozone scent of Aerithal's research quarter. A way-station provides refreshment and information for arriving travelers, and the Starwind Promenade is visible as a ribbon of activity extending northward into the city. The bridge behind stretches south toward Liraveth like a thread connecting two green worlds.

name: Canopy Bridge - Aerithal to Sylarien
description: The bridge connecting Aerithal and Sylarien takes the longest and most scenic route of the three inter-city connections, curving through open sky between the two groves. The bridge descends from Aerithal's height to Sylarien's slightly lower canopy, and the walk offers a gradual change in the ambient sound: from Aerithal's contemplative quiet to the first distant notes of Sylarien's ever-present music. Midway across, a wider platform allows travelers to pause and take in the full panorama of the three canopy cities, the Singing Ridges to the south, and on exceptional days the glint of the Great River far to the west.

name: Shadeharm Glade Approach - Edge of Twilight
description: The path south from the deep forest enters a region where the light changes abruptly, the normal green-gold twilight of the Windweave giving way to a permanent grey dusk that no amount of sunlight seems to penetrate. The trees here are different: twisted, dark-barked, their branches bare despite the season. A dissonant hum is audible at the edge of perception, a sound that makes Kin-sense users flinch with its wrongness. Circle of Deeproot wards line the path, their sigils glowing with active power, and the message is clear: this is as far as any sensible person should go. Ahead lies Shadeharm Glade, sealed by druidic power against whatever corruption lurks within.

name: Shadeharm Glade Approach - The Sealed Path
description: The path ends at a wall of living thorns, deliberately grown and maintained by the Circle of Deeproot to seal the approach to Shadeharm Glade. The thorns are thick as a Human's arm and bear leaves of an unhealthy black-green that seems to absorb light. Beyond the barrier, glimpses of the Glade are visible through gaps in the thorns: a valley shrouded in permanent twilight where the trees stand dead and the ground is covered in grey ash. The dissonant hum is stronger here, a pressure on Kin-sense that feels like listening to a conversation conducted in a language specifically designed to cause pain. The druidic wards burn brightest at this point, and the message they convey is unambiguous: do not enter.

name: Great River Road - Southern Custos Crossroads
description: A major crossroads where the Great River Road meets the southern approaches to Custos do Aeternos. A stone marker carved with the arms of the capital points north toward the city gates, while the road south stretches toward the farmlands and settlements of Twin Rivers. Travelers, merchants, and military patrols converge here, creating a busy intersection that serves as the unofficial boundary between the capital's territory and the wider region. The Great River is visible to the east, broad and slow, carrying the commercial traffic that binds the region together.

name: Giant's Teeth - Eastern Pass Descent
description: The eastern descent from the Giant's Teeth pass leads down through increasingly gentle terrain toward the distant plains of the Kinsweave region. The volcanic rock gives way to sedimentary stone and eventually to soil, and the harsh mountain vegetation softens into scrubby grassland. The wind at this elevation carries scents from the east: grassland, distant woodsmoke, and the dry mineral tang of the Kinsweave plateau. The boundary marker at the pass above is the last sign of Twin Rivers' territory, and travelers heading east are reminded to carry sufficient supplies for the crossing.

name: Northern Forest Road - Future Connection
description: The northern road from the Windweave Forest fades into an unmaintained track that disappears into the wild forests beyond Twin Rivers' settled territory. Druid deep-markers warn of untamed wilderness ahead, and the trees here have a wilder, more ancient quality than the managed forests near the settlements. The road is little more than a suggestion, its surface overgrown and its markers faded. Future development may extend Twin Rivers' reach in this direction, but for now the northern forest remains unexplored territory, its depths home to creatures and forces that the Circle of Deeproot monitors but does not attempt to control.

name: Windweave Canopy Descent
description: The great canopy lift platform serves as the primary connection between the ground-level settlements and the canopy cities three hundred feet above. The platform is a broad disc of living-wood reinforced with Pekakarlik iron framing, large enough to carry a dozen passengers and their cargo. The counterweight mechanism operates smoothly, the platform rising or descending at a steady pace that allows passengers to watch the forest layers pass: ground ferns, trunk-level moss and fungi, the first branching canopy, and finally the bright upper world of leaves and sky. The transition between ground and canopy is a journey between worlds, each level with its own ecology, its own light, and its own sounds.

name: Great River Road - Western Junction
description: A junction on the Great River Road where a lesser track branches west toward the territories of the Chainless Legion enclaves. The main road continues its north-south course between Custos and Greenford, but this western fork sees steady traffic from the trade envoys who manage the sensitive commerce between Twin Rivers' settlements and the Chainless communities. A small waypost manned by a single guard maintains a traveler's log, noting the passage of legitimate traders while watching for smugglers and trouble. The track west is rougher than the main road but passable in all seasons.

name: Bridge Cellar -- Stone Stair
description: A stone stair descends beneath Liraveth's Western Bridge Head, past the normal cellar-level of the river-house above and into the old flood-vaults the river-factor claims are abandoned. They are not abandoned. The stair ends in a warm, well-lit antechamber whose stone has been recently re-pointed.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Smuggler's Vault
description: A long vaulted chamber stacked with sealed crates marked for various buyers up and down the river. The inventory is impressive: Deceiver spell-foci in padded velvet; a wrapped bundle of Silentborn-made suppressor-collars; several sealed scroll-cases. Two hobgoblin 'caravan guards' lounge by the door, reading aloud from a ledger.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Merchant Facade
description: A front-office appointed as any reputable Eruskan river-factor's cellar-office would be: a writing-desk, shelves of ledger-books, a low stool for supplicants. Three Silentborn in merchant dress stand at the desk, reviewing papers with the quiet competence of people who have been at this work for years.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Porter's Quarters
description: A low-ceilinged bunk-room for the cell's goblin porters -- the workers who actually move the crates up and down river at night. Simple bedrolls, a shared chest, a cluttered pegboard of duty-schedules. The goblins here are paid fairly, which is unusual enough that they are fiercely loyal.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- River Dock Access
description: A concealed passage leading from the smuggler's vault out to a hidden dock beneath the bridge-head. Goods move through this passage by the cart-load, twice per week, by night. A small iron grate opens onto the river at the passage's far end.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Ledger Room
description: A proper accounting-office: high desks, tall stools, banks of pigeon-holes holding invoices and receipts. The cell's actual inventory is tracked here, in plain Kin-script, behind a veneer of ordinary river-trade paperwork. To a random inspector, everything would check out.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- The Broker's Office
description: A private office of good furniture and better taste. The desk is dark walnut; the rug is imported; the brandy on the sideboard is a Westerlin vintage of some note. At the desk sits a handsome, immaculately-dressed figure the Liraveth mercantile community knows as Madam Cerran the River-Factor. Her ears, if you look closely, are very slightly more pointed than a full Eruskan's.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Advisor's Room
description: A smaller private chamber used by the Broker's personal advisor -- an elderly Elf whose accent is from nowhere living Kin would recognize. He was smuggled through the Silence Breach as a young man, has served the Shattered Host for decades, and handles the coded correspondence with Dómnathar cells east of the river.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Strongbox Vault
description: A small reinforced room the Broker uses to store the cell's liquid reserves: three locked iron-bound strong-boxes holding Kin coinage, Dómnathar trade-bars of pure silver, and a fourth box containing emergency travel-papers in four different names.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Hidden Cache
description: A concealed alcove behind a pivot-panel in the Broker's office. Inside, wrapped in oilcloth, are the items the Broker does not declare even to the Spur Tower: a bundle of letters in her own hand (copies of reports she has made), a sheaf of coded names, and a small Deceiver-era device of unknown purpose.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Upriver Gate
description: A small iron-gated alcove leading to a concealed staircase up to a hidden exit somewhere in Liraveth's storage-quarter. The gate is unlocked by a mechanism operated from the Broker's desk. She uses this exit when she wishes to move through the city without being observed.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Guard Post
description: A modest sentry-post at the junction of the porters' quarters and the ledger room. A Silentborn in neutral traveling-clothes sits at a small table, reading a thin volume on Kin etiquette. His short-sword leans against the wall beside him, close enough to reach.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Contraband Store
description: A smaller strongroom holding the cell's most sensitive contraband: two unopened Deceiver-era crystal reliquaries, a rolled tapestry bearing the Shattered Host sigil, and a sealed clay jar whose contents no one in the cell has opened because the Broker has not yet told them what is inside.

name: Infiltrator Cell -- Escape Trapdoor
description: A trapdoor in the floor of the contraband store opens onto a narrow sloping passage that emerges -- eventually -- in a stand of reeds on the far side of the Great River. It is the Broker's final insurance. It has not been used.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Hidden Path
description: The Druidic Overlook's western edge conceals a narrow trail descending along the cliff-face, visible only to someone who knows precisely where to look. The trail ends at a carved stone threshold into the hillside -- an entrance disguised as a natural cave-mouth.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Greeting Hall
description: Past the stone threshold, a welcoming hall opens out, polished stone floor and whitewashed walls hung with modest tapestries of Hareem's rose-in-sword. Two acolytes in undyed robes stand at the welcome-desk, their sleeves bearing the small cut-string sigil. Their greeting, to unannounced visitors, is polite but firm: 'Your purpose, friend?'

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Philosophy Classroom
description: A long hall with rows of low reading-desks facing a raised dais. Currently empty; the morning lecture concluded an hour ago. Slate boards on the walls bear the lecture's outline -- a reading of Hareem's Cut-String doctrine interleaved with references to the Deceiver's Feat. The two are presented as philosophically identical.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Meditation Chamber
description: A circular room with cushions around the walls and a small brazier in the center, currently burning a light incense of frankincense and something harder to name. A Half-Dómnathar priest in blackened leather kneels in meditation -- or appears to. His breathing is measured. His eyes are open.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Practice Ring
description: A sand-floored circle sunk below the surrounding floor-level for easier observation. Racked along the walls are practice-weapons: wooden swords, padded daggers, weighted sparring-rods. Two young cultists currently spar in the ring, their instructor calling corrections from the ringside.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Scribe's Hall
description: A long narrow room where the Refuge's scribes copy out doctrinal texts for distribution to the congregation in town. High writing-desks, small reading-lamps, careful pots of black and red ink. Several works in progress are laid out on the desks; their contents, read closely, blur the line between Hareem's scriptures and Dómnathar propaganda.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- The Philosopher's Study
description: The Philosopher-in-Residence's private study. Walls of bound books; a reading-table piled with Dómnathar and Kin texts in equal proportion; a low divan for favored students; a silver decanter of excellent wine. At the table, the Philosopher himself looks up with the warm, practiced smile of a teacher recognizing the potential for a promising new student.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Favored Student's Chamber
description: A comfortable small cell adjoining the Philosopher's study, reserved for the student he currently finds most promising. Simple bedding, a writing-desk, a bookshelf with the student's own copies of the core texts. Currently occupied by a Silentborn student -- quiet, attentive, deeply loyal.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Second Student's Chamber
description: A second small cell, mirror of the first, occupied by another advanced student. This one is also Silentborn. The Philosopher's best students are always Silentborn. The congregation in town does not know this, but one day, they will.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Masters' Table
description: A small dining-hall where the Refuge's teachers and ranking students take meals together. The table is set for six but currently empty. The provisions, laid out on a sideboard, are excellent: good bread, strong cheese, a handsome roast. The cooking is done by servants who know better than to ask questions.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Library of Doctrine
description: The Refuge's main library: three walls of shelving floor-to-ceiling, the fourth a tall arched window looking east over the Riverwind Heights. A reading table; a globe; a rack of reading-lamps. The library is quiet. The collection is uncommonly good, especially its shelf of Dómnathar-era philosophical texts.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Guest Suite
description: A large guest-suite currently occupied by a Dark Dwarf dignitary visiting from the Marches to discuss weapons-sales arrangements. His quarters are spartan by dwarven standards but surprisingly well-appointed by Kin ones: a fine writing-desk, a half-read book on his cot, a standing weapons-rack holding a work-axe of unusual craftsmanship.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Training Hall of Shadow
description: A larger room than the practice ring, lit only by narrow skylights that cast bars of dim light across the floor. The hall is used to train the Refuge's advanced students in the more discrete arts. A Silentborn assassin stands in the deepest shadow along the east wall, running through a forms-drill with a real blade.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Shrine of the Feat
description: A small shrine set within the main building, ostensibly to Hareem of the Cut-String, whose rose-and-sword emblem hangs above the altar. Below the emblem, however, engraved into the altar-stone itself, is a second symbol: an iron circle, empty. Whoever kneels here prays to both faiths simultaneously.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Ritual Chamber
description: A stone-walled room reserved for the Refuge's more intimate rituals. A carved stone basin at the center holds water that is uncomfortably still. A human in cultist robes tends the basin, her face scarred with Flamewarg burns. She is the only full human in attendance today, and she is the least loyal to any Kin authority of anyone in the building.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Weapons Cache
description: A locked strongroom holding the Refuge's 'discrete' armament -- the weapons the students use in the Hall of Shadow and, eventually, in the field. Racks of dark-iron knives, crossbows with enclosed triggers, poisoned bolts in labeled cases. The locks are excellent.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Hidden Archive
description: A concealed library behind a pivot-shelf in the main library. Where the public collection holds Hareem's scriptures with subtle Dómnathar interpolations, this archive holds the raw Dómnathar source-texts, in the original script, together with the Refuge's complete correspondence with the Spur Tower.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Conclave Hall
description: A small round hall with seven carved chairs in a circle. The Refuge's ranking members gather here once per year to decide doctrine and operations. The chairs are numbered, each by the rank of its occupant. The First Chair is larger than the others and belongs to the Philosopher-in-Residence.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Deep Meditation Cells
description: A row of small individual cells for students undergoing extended meditation-retreats. Each cell contains only a mat, a small brass lamp, and a wall-shelf for a single book. Three cells are currently occupied by silent students; the rest are empty and dim.

name: Unstrung Refuge -- Escape Ladder
description: A concealed iron ladder climbs from the bottom of the meditation cells up through solid rock to an exit somewhere high on the Riverwind Heights cliff-face. The ladder is well-maintained and the upper hatch opens readily. The Philosopher has climbed this ladder three times in his career: once as practice, twice in earnest.
